name: Dual-Stage Compounder D3

The mixer is equipped with real-time rheological sensors and an adaptive control system that adjusts mixing parameters dynamically based on material properties, significantly reducing batch-to-batch variation and improving material quality.

name: Smart Tire Machine 3
description: This cutting-edge machine represents the pinnacle of tire assembly automation. It leverages advanced robotics, laser guidance systems, and real-time sensor feedback to build green tires with unparalleled precision. The process involves multiple stages, from bead setting and carcass building to tread application, all controlled by a central PLC. Key features include automatic component feeding, self-correction algorithms for alignment errors, and a cycle time optimized for high-volume production, typically completing a tire in less than 35 seconds. It handles a wide range of tire sizes and designs with minimal changeover time, thanks to its modular tooling system.

name: Production Site A
description: Production Site A is a cornerstone facility focused on the manufacturing of premium passenger car and light truck tires, with a specialization in ultra-high performance and all-season segments. The site operates three independent production lines, each equipped with state-of-the-art machinery, including VMI MAXX and Smart Tire Machines. A dedicated Mixing Department with multiple internal mixers and roll mills feeds these lines. The facility covers an area of over 500,000 square feet, employs approximately 1,200 personnel, and integrates advanced IoT sensors and AI-driven predictive maintenance systems across all critical assets. It adheres to strict environmental regulations and aims for zero-waste production by recycling rubber scrap and process water. Annual capacity exceeds 5 million tires.

name: Logistics Central Warehouse
description: This facility serves as the primary logistics hub for inbound raw materials, intermediate products, and outbound finished goods. It features high-bay automated storage and retrieval systems (AS/RS) for efficient inventory management, capable of holding thousands of tons of rubber compounds, textiles, and steel cords, as well as tens of thousands of finished tires. A fleet of AGVs and forklifts, coordinated by a Warehouse Management System (WMS), ensures seamless material flow to and from the production lines. The outbound section includes automated sorting, labeling, and loading docks for trucks, optimizing dispatch operations and minimizing shipping errors. Real-time tracking and inventory optimization algorithms are employed to reduce lead times and improve supply chain resilience.

name: Mixer 1
description: Mixer 1 is a high-capacity Banbury-type internal mixer, specifically engineered for the initial breakdown and compounding of natural and synthetic rubbers with various fillers, oils, and chemicals. Operating at high temperatures and pressures, it ensures thorough dispersion of ingredients to achieve homogeneous rubber compounds. The mixer is equipped with advanced process controls for temperature, rotor speed, and mixing duration, allowing for precise recipe execution. It features an automated weighing and feeding system for raw materials, minimizing human error and improving batch consistency. Integrated sensors monitor torque and energy consumption, providing data for predictive maintenance and process optimization. This mixer is a critical upstream component, directly impacting the physical properties and workability of the rubber supplied to subsequent departments.

name: TUM Quad Check

It processes approximately 180 tires per hour.

The machine employs sophisticated tension control systems and laser-guided alignment to ensure exceptional uniformity and balance in the 'green tire' structure. Its cycle time averages around 40 seconds, making it a critical throughput driver for the entire line. Data from integrated sensors feeds directly into a predictive maintenance system, enhancing uptime.

name: Mixer Main Line B
description: A large-scale Banbury-type mixer responsible for compounding bulk rubber batches with various additives like carbon black, silica, and curing agents. This high-intensity internal mixer operates under precise temperature and pressure controls to achieve homogeneous dispersion of ingredients, crucial for the final properties of the tire. It processes batches ranging from 250kg to 500kg, feeding several downstream calenders and extruders. The mixing process is highly exothermic, requiring efficient cooling systems, and its energy consumption is a significant operational factor, continuously monitored for optimization.

The site employs over 1,500 personnel and is striving for industry 4.0 integration across all its operations, focusing on energy efficiency and waste reduction initiatives to meet sustainability goals. It acts as a benchmark facility within the company's global network.
